title: eulophia pedicellata
intro: Eulophia pedicellata, also known as the orchid of the savannah, is a terrestrial orchid species native to tropical Africa. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Eulophia pedicellata include the treatment of various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and burns. Traditionally, the plant is prepared by boiling its roots and leaves in water to make a herbal tea, or by crushing its leaves to create a paste that can be applied topically.
